description: The construction of the Belo Monte Dam on the Xingu River in Brazil faced intense opposition from indigenous tribes and activists like Kayapo leader Raoni Metuktire. Despite global outcry and the endorsement of celebrities like James Cameron, the Brazilian government under Dilma Rousseff prioritized the energy project, which was completed in 2016.

key_events.description: James Cameron visits the Xingu basin to support the indigenous protest movement.
Brazilian government auctions off the construction rights despite pending legal injunctions.
Large-scale protests erupt in Altamira as construction begins on the diversion channel.
Brazilian courts overturn remaining environmental injunctions, effectively green-lighting completion.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling how high-profile celebrity advocacy struggles against state-backed industrial development.
